prompt: Which statement best describes a token in the context of large language models?
choices: A whole word, always exactly one per English word
A sub-word unit that text is broken into before the model processes it
A single letter of the alphabet
A complete sentence the model reads at once

prompt: Select ALL statements that are true about tokens and pricing.
choices: LLM usage is typically billed per token
Input tokens and output tokens are usually billed separately
One word always equals exactly one token
Context window limits are measured in tokens, not words

prompt: Using the rough rule of thumb that 100 tokens is about 75 words, roughly how many tokens is a 150-word prompt?

A Business Analyst is estimating the monthly cost of a feature that summarizes documents. A developer reports the prompts average 500 input tokens and the summaries average 300 output tokens per request. What should the BA do to produce an accurate cost estimate?
choices: Add input and output tokens together and apply a single price, since all tokens cost the same
Use only the input token count, because output is free
Cost input and output tokens separately, since providers usually price them differently
Ignore token counts and estimate cost purely by the number of words
